title: pilosella flagellaris
intro: Pilosella flagellaris, also known as mouse-ear hawkweed, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the dandelion family and is native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Pilosella flagellaris include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antiviral properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
